title: Convolution, sequence and attention roles

publication.changeSummary: Source-linked AI Foundations draft for Convolution, sequence and attention roles; all claims, examples, metrics, code, safety boundaries, and answer oracles require independent human review. sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A small image task starts with convolutional locality and compares a simple baseline before considering a large attention model. Your goal is to match a data structure and task to an architecture family and state limitations. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Supervised learning; Data and experimental workflow; Units, layers and activations; Dimensionality reduction. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Bounded operational model
sections.body: Convolution emphasizes local shared patterns, recurrence carries sequential state, and attention forms content-dependent interactions; architecture choice follows data structure, task, scale, latency, and evidence. Any capability or quality claim remains limited to an explicit task, population, conditions, baseline, and recorded evidence.

sections.heading: Failure boundary and non-claim
sections.body: These families overlap and combine, attention has substantial context cost, position needs representation, convolution does not ensure invariance, recurrence can lose long dependencies, and scale does not guarantee understanding. The learner must reject this overclaim: Transformers and attention are universally superior, understand sequence meaning, and remove context or compute limits.

includes: Recognize how locality, recurrence and attention encode different structure and compute trade-offs.
Match a data structure and task to an architecture family and state limitations.
